_comment: Example Cursor/Claude mcp.json for qactl's stdio MCP front.
Copy the entries you want into your client's mcp.json (e.g. ~/.cursor/mcp.json
or the project .cursor/mcp.json) and toggle the servers on.
Each server is a local stdio process: `qactl mcp <group>`. No HTTP ports,
no systemd units. Credentials resolve from the environment the client
launches qactl with (ATLASSIAN_* for jira/confluence, JENKINS_* for jenkins,
device creds via `qactl setup`); never put secrets in this file.
Per-group servers keep tool schemas small and let you enable only what you
need (e.g. skip the heavy ixia stack). Use the single 'qactl-all' entry
instead if you prefer one server exposing every group.
Heavy dnftp / large-artifact tools (device + netconf backups, tech-support,
tar-load, scale-deploy) and `setup` are intentionally CLI-only; run those as
`qactl cli ...` / `qactl nc ...` / `qactl setup` from a shell.
